pr_title: Use WSGIServer for serving Flask API [proposal][needs testing]
pr_body:
This small PR aims in replacing usage of Flask internal dev web server by [gevent's WSGIServer](http://www.gevent.org/gevent.pywsgi.html).
Flask's development server is [strongly advised against](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/0.12/api/#flask.Flask.run) for anything besides basic development execution. It doesn't support co-routines, and blocks while running (currently, that's the case, although we run it in it's own Greenlet).
As we're already using gevent loop in the whole application, it is interesting to put Flask server on this loop, using a WSGI-compliant webserver, and `gevent.wsgi.WSGServer` fits nicely, being a good tradeoff between as little configuration as possible, while taking full advantage of gevent's loop and async handling.
It'll also allow us to easily and  asynchronously serve static UI files, when it's pre-compiled and served through Flask' WSGI index endpoints (TODO).
Fix: https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/issues/467

I cherry picked Konrad's [commit](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/pull/400/commits/cc28a2a0196008e106f7eebdd702219a43290205) from the [automatic channel provisioning](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/pull/400/) PR since this is a fix we need in master asap to avoid some flaky failing tests.

pr_body:
Please review and merge if the test runs succeed.

Background:

  There were many indirect package dependencies that were not
  listed in the requirements and version constraint files.
  This resulted in an install failure when testing with
  PACKAGE_LEVEL=minimum, because the specified old versions of
  the packages that were listed, were mixed with the latest
  versions of the packages that were not listed.

Details of changes:

- Added all missing package dependencies as of 2016-12-08, with
  their versions as of that date as a minimum.
- Changed the minimum versions of already existing package
  dependencies to the versions as of 2016-12-08. This resulted
  sometimes in increasing the minimum version, and sometimes in
  decreasing it.

Signed-off-by: Andreas Maier <maiera@de.ibm.com>

pr_body: This removes some unused code. The only substantial change is to comment out the call to `flush_log()` in `worker.py`.

## What do these changes do?
Adds explicit dependencies for Tune, RLlib. 

Sitting on top of @ericl's #1288, so will wait for that to go through to merge.

## Related issue number
#1279

## What do these changes do?

This replaces the current actor checkpointing mechanism and extends it to support distributed actor handles. After executing a specified interval of tasks, the actor will execute a method to save the checkpoint. The checkpoint state includes the local scheduler's current task frontier for the actor, which is a mapping from handle ID to task counter and object dependency.

When an actor executes its first task (`__init__`), it checks first for a valid checkpoint. If one exists, the actor initializes its state from the saved checkpoint. It also resets the local scheduler's current task frontier. The task counters allow the local scheduler to filter out duplicate tasks submitted during reconstruction. The object dependencies allow the local scheduler to fulfill execution dependencies for tasks that execute after the checkpoint.

## Related issue number

None

This PR will serve to add a command line and programmatic (Python) way to convert from a Dockerfile to a SIngularity Recipe. To provide the reviewer with documentation and notes that are easier to read, please see the rendered docs on the Github pages from the vsoch branch of the equivalent repository:

https://vsoch.github.io/singularity-cli/recipes

We will need lots of help testing! Specifically, there are a lot of different formats / options that might go into a Dockerfile, and I only tested a very limited set. To help with this review, please do the following:

### Install from vsoch master
```
git clone https://www.github.com/vsoch/singularity-cli
cd singularity-cli
python setup.py install
```
The above will place an executable `spython` in your local bin. The functions you import are from this module. See the documentation (next) for more details.

### Documentation
Read the documentation and likely it will be easiest to test via the command line first. Then you have a few options. You can:
  - Report issues here! I can address and update the vsoch/master branch, and then alert you when it's ready for re-test. or 
 - **even better** submit a tiny PR to the vsoch/master directly, it will be added here when it's merged.

Once you are happy with general recipe parsing, please continue to test the (from within python) functions.

## Notes

- testing / CI would be greatly appreciated!

pr_title: Bug 1441799 - Support number variant keys in BaseNode.equals
pr_body:
`BaseNode.equals` sorts order-agnostic fields such as attributes and variants in order to ensure the equality comparison works correctly even if the order of the elements differs. The code used to assume all variant keys were `VariantName` instances. We need to support numbers as keys as well.
